title: 8 Returning AFT Transactions via the Batch Return Service â BC Only
body_text:
8	Returning AFT Transactions via the Batch Return Service â BC Only
8.1	Overview of Returning AFT Transactions via the Batch Return Service
The Batch Return Service describes the electronic return of AFT Transactions via a financial institutionâs in-house or third-party processing system. Once an I & J file is created, it is sent to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. The transaction is returned to the originating financial institution and, if arranged, an offsetting entry is posted to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account.
Central 1 can neither support the set-up of transactions and users on this system nor its day-to-day operation.
8.2	File Specifications
The I & J file must follow the specifications provided in the I & J Batch Returns File Specifications (BC Only), Form 6273.
Related Information
I & J Batch Returns File Specifications,
Form 6273
(BC Only)
8.3	Deadline for Processing I & J Files
I & J files must be successfully received at Central 1 by 4:00 pm PT. Files received after the deadline wonât be processed until the following day.
8.4	Installing and Setting Up the Batch Return Service
Consult a technical staff or third-party service provider for information on installing and setting up the returned item software.
8.5	Converting to Alternate AFT Returns Processing Software
Notify Central 1 if the financial institution plans to convert to alternate AFT returns processing software. If the financial institution doesnât notify Central 1 of the change, I and J files can be rejected. To notify Central 1, complete the I & J Returns â System Conversion, Form 1892, and forward it to Central 1 at least 1 week before the conversion.
Note:	Ensure that the form is signed by an authorized financial institution Signing Officer(s).
Completion of the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: I & J Returns â System Conversion, Form 1892
Related Information
I & J Returns â System Conversion,
Form 1892
(BC Only)
8.6	Processing Returns via the Batch Return Service
To compare the total number and value of returned items entered in the returned item software with a secondary source, use one of the following:
A tape listing of returns from the Overdraft report
The Request to Process AFT Returns, Form 1249, (or a similar form)
See Figure 2. Compare totals and item counts to those reported on the system. Donât forward this information to Central 1.
Figure 2: Request to Process AFT Returns, Form 1249
Process the returns as instructed by the software provider. Always ensure that you follow
CPA
Rules and time frames. See Chapter 1, Overview of AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions.
Verify the accuracy of the returns before generating the I & J File.
Related Information
Chapter 1
, Overview of AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions
Request to Process AFT Returns,
Form 1249
8.7	Sending the I & J File to Central 1
Users who upload I & J Files to Central 1 must have access to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange and the RETP folder. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on obtaining access to the File and Report Exchange.
Before sending the first I & J return file, complete the FTP User Request, Form 2010, to set up the RETP subdirectory for a user. Completion of the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: FTP User Request, Form 2010
Uploading the I & J File to Central 1
Connect to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange and go to the RETP subdirectory.
Browse to find the I & J file you want to send.
Upload the file to Central 1 according to the specifications of the FTP Client or alternate connection method. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on uploading files online via the Client Centre.
Confirming that the I & J File Uploaded Successfully
Ensure that the I & J File is successfully sent to Central 1 by 3:00 pm, PT.
Check that the following files are in the RETP directory a few minutes after uploading the file:
â
.LST
â is always created and contains a message advising if the file was successful
â
.BAK
â is a back-up of the original I & J file
â
.ERR
â is created only if an error exists in the file. It will advise you to refer to the .LST file for the error message.
Open the .LST file to confirm that the file was successfully uploaded. If an error occurred, make the appropriate correction and resend the file.
Confirming Settlement and Processing Returns
Since financial institutions are ultimately responsible for ensuring that each return is processed, it is recommended that financial institution staff verify that the totals indicated on the reports below match the expected file total.
MERG Reports
â
Batch Returns User List (ICBR/UCBR) â Lists accepted transactions.
â
Batch Returns Error List (ICBE/UCBE) â Lists transactions rejected during the first edit.
Daily Journal Report â The total of AFT Transactions, and the offsetting entry, will be netted. If the net total entry is balanced, meaning 0, there is no posting. If the net total entry doesnât equal 0, either a debit or a credit entry will be posted to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account. Credits and Debits are coded as AR (AFT Returns).
See the AFT Reports Part for more information on reports. If the information from the MERG Reports and/or the Daily Journal doesnât match the financial institutionâs records, contact Central 1 Support immediately.
Client Support Services
